name: Almond-Raisin Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Lightly toasted, coarsely chopped almonds
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: COOKING RIGHT SHOW #CR9649
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Butter a baking sheet or line it with parchment.
In a large bowl, beat the egg yolks with 3/4 cup of sugar until light and the sugar is dissolved. Stir in the melted butter, almonds, raisins, and orange peel. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until they just begin to form peaks; gradually beat in the remaining 3/4 cup sugar until the whites form stiff peaks.
Sift together the flour and baking powder. Alternately fold 1/3 of the flour into the yolk mixture, then 1/3 of the egg whites; repeat until well combined. The dough will be firm and slightly sticky. If needed, add more flour to achieve a firm consistency. Divide the dough into two logs about 1-1/2 inches wide. Place on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until lightly browned and firm to the touch.
Remove from oven and set the baking sheet on a rack for 10 minutes. On a cutting board, cut each log diagonally into slices 1/2 inch wide. Return the slices to the baking sheet and bake for an additional 5 to 7 minutes on each side or until lightly browned and crisp. Allow to cool completely before storing in airtight containers.
Yield: About 36 biscotti
